title: Benchmark Manifest
description: Frozen, versioned description of a benchmark set. Raw map/replay data lives outside git; this manifest pins what the set refers to by content hash. See docs/benchmark-policy.md.

schema_version.description: Version of the manifest schema itself. Current release: 1.

benchmark_id.description: Stable id for the benchmark set, independent of version. Must match the filename stem prefix (i.e. <benchmark_id>-v<version>.yaml).

version.description: Monotonic integer version. Starts at 1. Changes require a new file; editing an existing version in place is forbidden.

category.description: One of the Phase 1 seed categories. See docs/benchmark-policy.md.

ingestion_snapshot.description: The ingestion snapshot id that this manifest resolves its entries against.

released_at.description: ISO 8601 date (YYYY-MM-DD) on which this version was frozen.

author.description: Email or stable handle of the person who released this version.

rationale.description: Why this benchmark exists and what it measures. Must be substantive — the review step rejects empty or generic rationale.

supersedes.description: If this version replaces a previous version, name it here as <benchmark_id>-v<N>.

tags.description: Optional labels. Must not encode validity or score — those are per-entry labels.

entries.description: Non-empty list of benchmark entries. Each entry pins a map by canonical id and content hash.

map_id.description: Canonical map id from the ingestion snapshot named at the top level.

content_hash.description: SHA-256 of the referenced raw artifact, lowercase hex. Verified at load time.

role.description: How the entry is used during evaluation.

label.description: Hand-curated labels. TMX self-reported tags are not permitted as ground truth. Schema is category-specific and is validated downstream — only structural presence is enforced here.

comment.description: Optional per-entry note. Kept in-manifest, not in a sidecar file.
